tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, ketonuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting significant renal impairment with possible acute or chronic kidney injury. Mild metabolic acidosis is indicated by low CO₂. No evidence of urinary tract infection is seen. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) further support renal dysfunction. Prompt nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
